a11yBodyPageHeader: Individualized Accessibility Process for Resource Creators
a11yBodyIntroGraph:
At edX, we seek to understand and respect the unique needs and perspectives of the edX global community.
      We value every resource team and are committed to expanding access to all, including resource team creators and authors with
      disabilities. To that end, we have adopted a {communityAccessibilityLink} and this process to allow resource team creators
      and authors to request assistance if they are unable to develop and post content on our platform via Studio because of their
      disabilities.
a11yBodyStepsHeader: Resource team creators and authors needing such assistance should take the following steps:
a11yBodyOngoingSupport: EdX will provide ongoing technical support as needed and will address any additional issues that arise after the initial resource creation.
a11yBodyTimeConstraints: how soon you need access and for how long (e.g., a planned resource start date or in connection with a resource-related deadline such as a final essay).
course-authoring.advanced-settings.alert: This resource has protected exam setting that are incomplete or invalid.
course-authoring.advanced-settings.sideb: Advanced settings control specific resource functionality. On this page, you can edit manual policies, which are JSON-based key and value pairs that control specific resource settings.
course-authoring.certificates.details.co: Specify an alternative to the official resource title to display on certificates. Leave blank to use the official resource title.
course-authoring.certificates.sidebar.wo: Specify a resource title to use on the certificate if the resource\
To view a sample certificate, choose a resource mode and select {strongText}.
course-authoring.certificates.sidebar.is: To begin issuing resource certificates, a resource team member with either the Staff or Admin role selects {strongText}. Only resource team members with these roles can edit or delete an activated certificate.
{strongText} delete certificates after a resource has started; learners who have already earned certificates will no longer be able to access them.
course-authoring.certificates.without.mo: This resource does not use a mode that offers certificates.
welcomeMessageLongDescription: Personally welcome learners into your resource and prepare learners for a positive resource experience.
courseDatesLongDescription: Establish your resource schedule, including when the resource starts and ends.
assignmentDeadlinesLongDescription: Ensure all assignment deadlines are between resource start and end dates.
assignmentDeadlinesComment: The following assignments have deadlines that do not fall between resource start and end date:
videoDurationLongDescription: Learners engage best with short videos followed by opportunities to practice. Ensure that 80% or more of resource videos are less than 10 minutes long.
mobileFriendlyVideoLongDescription: Mobile-friendly videos can be viewed across all supported devices. Ensure that at least 90% of resource videos are mobile friendly by uploading resource videos to the edX video pipeline.
weeklyHighlightsLongDescription: Enable and specify weekly highlights to keep learners engaged and on track in your resource.
unitDepthLongDescription: Breaking up resource content into manageable pieces promotes learner engagement. We recommend units contain no more than three components.
proctoringEmailLongDescription: Resources using Proctortrack require an escalation email. Ensure learners and Support can contact your resource team regarding proctoring issues (e.g. appeals, exam resets, etc).
course-authoring.course-outline.status-b: When you enable resource highlight emails, learners automatically receive email messages for each section that has highlights. You cannot disable highlights after you start sending them.

course-authoring.course-rerun.form.descr: Provide identifying information for this re-run of the resource. The original resource is not affected in any way by a re-run. {strong}
Note: Together, the organization, resource number, and resource run must uniquely identify this new resource instance.
course-authoring.course-rerun.sidebar.se: When will my resource re-run start?
The new resource is set to start on
What transfers from the original resource?
The new resource has the same resource outline and content as the original resource. All problems, videos, announcements, and other files are duplicated to the new resource.
What does not transfer from the original resource?
You are the only member of the new resource\

Adding team members makes resource authoring collaborative. Users must be signed up for Studio and have an active account.

course-authoring.course-team.sidebar.abo: Resource team members with the Staff role are resource co-authors. They have full writing and editing privileges on all resource content.
Admins are resource team members who can add and remove other resource team members.
All resource team members can access content in Studio, the LMS, and Insights, but are not automatically enrolled in the resource.

course-authoring.custom-pages.note:
Note: Pages are publicly visible. If users know the URL
      of a page, they can view the page even if they are not registered for
      or logged in to your resource.
course-authoring.custom-pages.pageExplan:
Pages are listed horizontally at the top of your resource. Default pages (Home, Resource, Discussion, Wiki, and Progress)
      are followed by textbooks and custom pages that you create.
course-authoring.custom-pages.customPage:
You can create and edit custom pages to probide students with additional resource content. For example, you can create
      pages for the grading policy, resource slide, and a resource calendar.
course-authoring.custom-pages.studentVie: How do pages look to students in my resource?
Students see the default and custom pages at the top of your resource and use the links to navigate.
authoring.problemEditor.advanceProblem.s:
{supportStatus,  select,
      Provisional {Provisionally supported tools might lack the robustness of functionality
        that your resources require. edX does not have control over the quality of the software,
        or of the content that can be provided using these tools.
        \n \n
        Test these tools thoroughly before using them in your resource, especially in graded
        sections. Complete documentstion might not be available for provisionally supported
        tools, or documentation might be available from sources other than edX.}
      Not_supported {Tools with no support are not maintained by edX, and might be deprecated
        in the future. They are not recommened for use in resources due to non-compliance with one
        or more of the base requirements, such as testing, accessibility, internationalization,
        and documentation.}
      other { } 
    }

course-authoring.files-and-uploads.file-:
By default, anyone can access a file you upload if
      they know the web URL, even if they are not enrolled in your resource.
      You can prevent outside access to a file by locking the file. When
      you lock a file, the web URL only allows learners who are enrolled
      in your resource and signed in to access the file.

course-authoring.grading-settings.credit: % Must be greater than or equal to the resource passing grade
course-authoring.grading-settings.sideba: You can use the slider under Overall Grade Range to specify whether your resource is pass/fail or graded by letter, and to establish the thresholds for each grade.
You can specify whether your resource offers students a grace period for late assignments.
course-authoring.group-configurations.si: If you have cohorts enabled in your resource, you can use content groups to create cohort-specific courseware. In other words, you can customize the content that particular cohorts see in your resource.

Each content group that you create can be associated with one or more cohorts. In addition to making resource content available to all learners, you can restrict access to some content to learners in specific content groups. Only learners in the cohorts that are associated with the specified content groups see the additional content.

Use experiment group configurations if you are conducting content experiments, also known as A/B testing, in your resource. Experiment group configurations define how many groups of learners are in a content experiment. When you create a content experiment for a resource, you select the group configuration to use.

Enrollment track groups allow you to offer different resource content to learners in each enrollment track. Learners enrolled in each enrollment track in your resource are automatically included in the corresponding enrollment track group.
On unit pages in the resource outline, you can restrict access to components to learners based on their enrollment track.
You cannot edit enrollment track groups, but you can expand each group to view details of the resource content that is designated for learners in the group.

course-authoring.import.description1: Be sure you want to import a resource before continuing. The contents of the imported resource will replace the contents of the existing resource. You cannot undo a resource import. Before you proceed, we recommend that you export the current resource, so that you have a backup copy of it.
course-authoring.import.description2: The resource that you import must be in a .tar.gz file (that is, a .tar file compressed with GNU Zip). This .tar.gz file must contain a resource.xml file. It may also contain other files.

authoring.discussions.stuffOnlyConfigGui: To fully configure {providerName} will also require sharing usernames and emails for learners and resource team. Please contact your edX project coordinator to enable PII sharing for this resource.
authoring.discussions.builtIn.divideCour: Choose which of your general resource-wide discussion topics you would like to divide.
authoring.discussions.builtIn.groupInCon: Learners will be able to view any post in the sub-section no matter which unit page they are viewing. While this is not recommended, if your resource has short learning sequences or low enrollment grouping may increase engagement.
authoring.discussions.builtIn.allowAnony: Learners will be able to post anonymously to other peers but all posts will be visible to resource staff.
authoring.discussions.discussionTopics.h: Discussions can include general topics not contained to the resource structure. All resources have a general topic by default.
authoring.discussions.discussionTopicDel: edX recommends that you do not delete discussion topics once your resource is running.

authoring.discussions.appList.appDescrip: Start conversations with other learners, ask questions, and interact with other learners in the resource.
Enable participation in discussion topics alongside resource content.
Yellowdig offers educators a gameful learning digital solution to improve student engagement by building learning communities for any resource modality.
course-authoring.pages-resources.descrip: Keep learners engaged and on track throughout the resource.
Encourage participation and engagement in your resource with discussions.
Allow learners to highlight passages and make notes right in the resource.
Enable learners to access, and collaborate on resource-related information.

Create and manage a library of resource readings, textbooks, and chapters.
Provide additional resource content and resources with custom pages.

course-authoring.schedule.pacing.radio.i: Instructor-paced resources progress at the pace that the resource author sets. You can configure release dates for resource content and due dates for assignments.
course-authoring.schedule.pacing.radio.s: Self-paced resources offer suggested due dates for assignments or exams based on the learner’s enrollment date and the expected resource duration. These resources offer learners flexibility to modify the assignment dates as needed.

course-authoring.schedule.schedule-secti: Certificates are awarded at the end of a resource run

In all configurations of this setting, certificates are generated for learners as soon as they achieve the passing threshold in the resource (which can occur before a final assignment based on resource design).

Learners can access their certificate as soon as they achieve a passing grade above the resource grade threshold. Note: learners can achieve a passing grade before encountering all assignments in some resource configurations.
On resource end date
Learners with passing grades can access their certificate once the end date of the resource has elapsed.
A date after the resource end date
End date of resource
